topic: In China employee termination: position paper for foreign investors on legal defensibility and payroll governance
primary_keyword: China employee termination position paper foreign investors

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Primary legal baseline for position arguments on route legitimacy and consequence risk.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Supports implementation-level arguments in board/investor governance policies.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Provides dispute-lens support for evidence and consistency governance stance.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Anchors payroll and statutory closure obligations in governance recommendations.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Provides practical interpretation for foreign investors/operators implementing policy stance.

source_replacement_notes: Rotated from role-relay governance map to position-paper format while preserving high-confidence legal source hierarchy.
Excluded lower-fit tax-first and generic comparative commentary to keep investor-position argument focused on China legal defensibility.
Retained China Briefing as practical multinational interpretation layer.
consensus: Foreign investors should treat China termination as a governance-level legal risk, not a local HR administrative issue.
Policy should enforce legal-route-first sequencing and cross-function consistency checks.
Board-level oversight is most effective when it measures defensibility quality, not only closure speed.
uncertainties: City-level implementation and evidentiary emphasis vary.
Complex payment treatment in edge cases may require local confirmation.
Official English texts may not fully capture latest Chinese-language implementation nuance.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for position-paper format because sources support legal authority, dispute-risk framing, and practical foreign-operator application.
